tutorial-agent-assignment-body: Open the Assignment tab and assign an agent to start the facility working. An agent's effect only applies to the facility it is assigned to.
tutorial-agent-assignment-tip: Exception: an agent assigned inside the Super Terminal itself applies its effect to every facility at once, not just one.
tutorial-ap-system-desc: AP (Action Points) starts at 1 and increases by 1 each turn, up to 8. Playing cards costs AP. Higher AP means more powerful actions.
tutorial-battle-waiting-body: Tips minimized. Finish this fight first.
tutorial-boss-battle-waiting-body: Tips minimized. Finish this boss fight first.
tutorial-death-protection-toast: {{heroName}} took lethal damage but will keep fighting for you.
tutorial-dungeon-desc: A specially designed dungeon for new players. Enemies are very weak — perfect for learning the basics.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-items: Use items from your inventory to restore HP or AP during exploration.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-map: Tap "Full Map" to see all layers of the dungeon.
tutorial-dungeon-tips-share: Public dungeons support up to 3 players.
tutorial-flee-not-available: Flee is not available in the tutorial dungeon.
tutorial-node-desc-ap: Restores a significant amount of AP.
tutorial-node-desc-battle: Face enemies in turn-based card combat.
tutorial-node-desc-boss: The final boss. Defeat it to complete the dungeon.
tutorial-node-desc-elite-reward: Exchange more Reward Tokens for higher-tier items.
tutorial-node-desc-heal: Restores a significant amount of HP to all heroes.
tutorial-node-desc-lore: Discover background lore about the world of Terminal Ark.
tutorial-node-desc-minor-ap: Restores a small amount of AP.
tutorial-node-desc-minor-heal: Restores a small amount of HP to all heroes.
tutorial-node-desc-start: The entry point of the dungeon. Begin your exploration here.
tutorial-node-desc-trap: Opening a reward node may reveal a trap box. Be prepared!
tutorial-skip-confirm-line-1: To help you understand the game better, skipping the tutorial is not recommended.
tutorial-skip-confirm-line-2: Once skipped, this guide cannot be opened again.
tutorial-step-10-body: AP (Action Points) refills each turn in battle and is used to play cards. Restoring AP now gives you an advantage.
tutorial-step-11-body: This node restores a larger amount of AP. Keep your AP high to stay flexible in battle.
tutorial-step-12-body: Click the battle node to fight enemies. Defeating them earns reward tokens.
tutorial-step-12-inline-type-tip: Type hint: same-type defense only takes 50% damage, while cross-type attacks still deal 100% damage.
tutorial-step-12a-body: The top area shows enemy information and the upcoming action order. After selecting a usable attack card, tap an enemy to use it; without a selected card, tap an enemy to inspect details.
tutorial-step-12ab-body: At the start of each unit's new turn, it automatically performs one basic action first. The 'Next Basic Action' and 'Basic Action Target' markers show who will strike or heal next, and who the target will be.
tutorial-step-12ab-tip: Tip: Basic actions do not cost AP, but they still scale with the unit's current stats, effects, and role setup.
tutorial-step-12b-body: This is your hand. Pick a card, then tap a target.
tutorial-step-12c-body: Enable "Show Card Details" to preview a card's full effect before using it.
tutorial-step-12d-body: Your heroes are shown here. For healing, shielding, or support cards, select the card first and then tap the allied hero you want to target.
tutorial-step-12e-body: The battle log records all events this fight. Tap "View All" for the full history.
tutorial-step-12f-body: AP: total action points | Action counter | End Action | Deck | Flee
tutorial-step-12f-tip-flee: Flee: Causes a random allied hero to die (HP → 0). If only 1 hero remains, fleeing causes total defeat.
tutorial-step-12h-body: It is your turn to control the currently acting hero. Select one of that hero's usable cards from your hand, then tap an enemy or ally to cast it; if you want to save AP or have no playable cards, tap "End Action".
tutorial-step-13-body: After battle your heroes may be injured. This Minor Heal node restores some HP — use it.
tutorial-step-13-tip-item: Tip: You can also use consumable items in your inventory to restore HP during a dungeon.
tutorial-step-14-body: This Heal node restores a larger amount of HP. Get your heroes ready for the boss ahead.
tutorial-step-15-body: Tap the highlighted boss node to start the final fight.
tutorial-step-16-body: Well done! Tap "Leave Dungeon" to exit, then confirm in the dialog.
tutorial-step-18-body: Open the Wasteland Development Terminal first. This is where you plan facilities, dispatch agents, and establish your frontline operations network.
tutorial-step-19-body: Inside the Wasteland Development Terminal, you can build activity facilities to gain power, orders, and processing capacity.
tutorial-step-19-tip: Build the core facilities first, then add agents and long-term schedules later.
tutorial-step-1a-body: Tap here to open the Terminal Apps menu.
tutorial-step-1c-body: Claim the starter pack. Your 3 starting heroes will be added to your team automatically.
tutorial-step-2-body: Tap "City Mode" to enter the city.
tutorial-step-3-body: Tap "Terminal Ark Lower Deck Outskirts" to enter it.
tutorial-step-4-body: Tap "Abandoned Carnival Bar" to enter it.
tutorial-step-6-body: Create a tutorial dungeon to begin your adventure.
tutorial-step-8a-body: This panel shows the selected node's details — type, enemies, and available actions.
tutorial-step-8b-body: This section shows all available nodes on the current layer. Tap once to select, tap again to enter.
tutorial-step-8c-body: This section shows your heroes' current HP and status.
tutorial-step-8d-body: The action bar shows your AP, reward tokens, and current layer. Use "Leave" to exit the dungeon.
tutorial-step-9-body: Tap the Start node to begin exploring.
tutorial-team-auto-assigned: Mira, Brakka, and Zephyr have joined your team!
tutorial-type-advantage-same: Same-type defense: only take 80% damage (20% reduced)
tutorial-wasteland-build-power-body: Finally, build a Nuclear Power Plant. Your other facilities need enough power supply to keep running steadily.
tutorial-wasteland-build-power-tip: A Nuclear Power Plant provides baseline power even without assigned agents, making it one of the most important early activity facilities.
tutorial-wasteland-build-processing-body: Next, build a Processing Station in the following activity slot. You will later use it to produce materials specifically needed to fulfill Trade Station orders.
tutorial-wasteland-build-trading-body: First, build your first temporary outpost here: the Trading Station. It will automatically acquire orders and bring you Memory and other rewards.
tutorial-wasteland-detail-body: Good. Now open one of the facilities you just built and inspect its detail screen. This is where you check status, actions, and assignment info.
tutorial-wasteland-map-body: This is the signal map of the Wasteland Development Terminal. Here you can inspect available zones and decide where your next facility should be built.
tutorial-wasteland-modify-mode-body: Turn on Modify Mode first. Once active, building points will appear and you can construct new facilities in buildable slots.
tutorial-welcome-body: You cleared your first dungeon and established the basic facilities of the Wasteland Development Terminal. From here on, Terminal Ark opens up with new contracts, story routes, and deeper dungeons.
tutorial-welcome-gacha-hint-sub: Head to the Signal Hijack system to decode new heroes and equipment.
tutorial-welcome-tip-equip: Keep upgrading heroes and equipping gear. Harder dungeons demand a properly prepared squad.
tutorial-welcome-tip-network: Joining a Terminal User Network gives the Execution Machine extra time reduction, plus support heroes and shared intel.
tutorial-welcome-tip-story: Play Story Mode to advance the world and earn more workstation and growth materials.
tutorial-welcome-tip-workstation: When you need long-term planning, return to the Wasteland Development Terminal to adjust facilities and agent assignments.
tutorial-welcome-workstation-hint: The workstation sets the rhythm of survival
tutorial-welcome-workstation-hint-sub: Use the Execution Machine for long decryption jobs, and the Manufacturing Station for instant cluster and amplifier crafting.
tutorial-workstation-guide-body: Your first simulation is complete. Return to the terminal and I will guide you through building the first frontline facilities in the Wasteland Development Terminal.
tutorial-workstation-manufacturing-stati: The Manufacturing Station synthesizes materials and common terminal resources, making it a key logistics node for expansion.
tutorial-workstation-power-plant-body: The Nuclear Power Plant provides power supply so your other facilities can keep operating.
tutorial-workstation-rest-room-body: The Rest Room restores the mood of assigned agents. Once mood is depleted, many facilities will stop functioning.
tutorial-workstation-super-terminal-body: The Super Terminal is the signal core of the Wasteland Development Terminal. Upgrading it unlocks deeper facility zones.
tutorial-workstation-trading-station-bod: The Trading Station automatically acquires orders. Once you gather the required items, you can deliver them for rewards.
